name: No Unilateral Contract Modification by Partner
description: No clause may give the partner a unilateral right to change pricing, commercial terms, or scope. All amendments must be mutually agreed and signed by both parties.
violation_threshold: Any clause granting partner unilateral right to modify pricing, scope, or commercial terms

name: Payment Terms Standard
description: Nexus standard payment term is 30 days upon invoice receipt. 14 days acceptable if commercially necessary. Under 14 days requires Finance sign-off. For revenue-generating contracts, collection terms must always be shorter than payment terms.
violation_threshold: Payment terms shorter than 14 days without Finance sign-off. Collection terms longer than or equal to payment terms.

name: Liability Cap Must Be Proportionate and Mutual
description: Liability caps must reflect the deal's risk/benefit. Carve-outs that apply only to Nexus (asymmetric) require Legal sign-off. Non-limitable categories always apply: gross negligence, willful misconduct, death/injury, fraud, IP infringement, data protection violations.
violation_threshold: Asymmetric carve-out applied exclusively to Nexus, or exclusion of mandatory non-limitable categories

name: Governing Law Must Match Jurisdiction and Meet Regional Standards
description: Governing law and jurisdiction must always be from the same country. For EU-based partners: German law and Berlin courts preferred; partner's local law acceptable if they insist. Any change to governing law or jurisdiction requires Legal sign-off.
violation_threshold: Governing law and jurisdiction from different countries. Or jurisdiction changed without Legal sign-off.

name: Data Processing Agreement Required Where Applicable
description: Where Nexus shares traveler personal data with a supplier processing it on Nexus's behalf, a DPA must exist confirming processor relationship. Removal of DPA requirement or shift to independent controller model requires Legal review.
violation_threshold: Removal of processor relationship or DPA requirement without Legal sign-off

name: SLA P1 Resolution Must Be Defined and Enforceable
description: For TSP agreements and critical technical providers, SLAs must include measurable metrics with clear breach triggers and termination rights. Degradation of SLA terms requires Product/Tech and CS review. P1 resolution time increases are material.
violation_threshold: P1 resolution time increased. SLA degradation without Product/Tech review.

name: Credit Facility Reductions Require Finance Approval
description: Any reduction to Nexus's operating credit facility, or introduction of conditions allowing the partner to unilaterally reduce it, requires Finance sign-off.
violation_threshold: Credit facility reduced or made contingent on volume without Finance sign-off

name: Termination for SLA Breach Must Not Require Remediation Plan from Nexus
description: Nexus must retain immediate or short-notice termination rights for material SLA breach. Any clause requiring Nexus to provide a formal remediation plan to the partner before exercising termination rights is not acceptable — this reverses the standard and undermines Nexus's exit rights.
violation_threshold: Nexus's SLA-based termination right conditioned on Nexus providing a remediation plan or cure period to the partner

name: Exclusivity on Nexus Is Not Permitted
description: Nexus must not agree to exclusivity clauses committing to work exclusively with a partner. Volume commitments may be offered as a compromise only with C-level, Director of Legal, and CEO sign-off.
violation_threshold: Any clause requiring Nexus to work exclusively with the partner

name: Commission and Revenue Terms Must Be Mutually Agreed
description: Commercial terms and pricing are material and must always be negotiated and mutually agreed. Any clause allowing the partner to unilaterally change commission rates or revenue share is not acceptable.
violation_threshold: Any clause giving partner unilateral right to change commission or revenue terms
